name: clear diagnostic event-log
detail: Clear the diagnostic event logs for a specific switch module or event type.
syntax: clear diagnostic event-log [event-type {error | info | warning} | switch {switch_num module module_num | all [event-type {error | info | warning}]}]

params.name: event-type error
params.description: Clears the error events.

params.name: event-type info
params.description: Clears the informative events.

params.name: event-type warning
params.description: Clears the warning events.

params.name: switch num
params.description: Clears the events for a specific switch.

params.name: module num
params.description: Clears the events for a specific module.

params.name: switch all
params.description: Clears all the event logs from all the switches.

name: clear secure-stackwise-virtual interface
detail: Clear the Secure StackWise Virtual interface statistics counters.

name: diagnostic monitor
detail: Configure health-monitoring diagnostic testing.
syntax: diagnostic monitor interval switch number module number test {name | test-id | test-id-range | all} hh:mm:ss milliseconds day [cardindex number] diagnostic monitor switch number module number test {name | test-id | test-id-range | all} [cardindex number] diagnostic monitor threshold switch number module number test {name | test-id | test-id-range | all} failure count count [days number | hours number | milliseconds number | minutes number | runs number | seconds number] cardindex number all} [cardindex number] all}[cardindex number] | all} { failure count [[count [days number | hours number | milliseconds number | minutes number | runs number | seconds number] | cardindex number] | cardindex number]}
noForm: no diagnostic monitor interval switch number module number test {name | test-id | test-id-range | no diagnostic monitor switch number module number test {name | test-id | test-id-range | no diagnostic monitor threshold switch number module number test {name | test-id | test-id-range

params.name: interval
params.description: Configures the interval between tests.

params.name: switch number
params.description: Specifies the switch number, which is the stack member number. If the switch is a standalone switch, the switch number is 1. If the switch is in a stack, the range is from 1 to 9, depending on the switch member numbers in the stack. This keyword is supported only on on stacking-capable switches.

params.name: test
params.description: Specifies the tests to be run.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the test.

params.name: test-id
params.description: ID number of the test.

params.name: test-id-range
params.description: Range of test ID numbers. Enter the range as integers separated by a comma and a hyphen (for example, 1,3-6 specifies test IDs 1, 3, 4, 5, and 6).

params.name: all
params.description: Specifies all the diagnostic tests.

params.name: hh:mm:ss
params.description: Monitoring interval, in hours, minutes, and seconds. Enter the hours from 0 to 24, minutes from 0 to 60, and seconds from 0 to 60. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 26.x.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 199 High Availability diagnostic monitor

params.name: milliseconds
params.description: Monitoring interval, in milliseconds (ms). Enter the test time, in milliseconds, from 0 to 999.

params.name: day
params.description: Monitoring interval, in days. Enter the number of days between test, from 0 to 20.

params.name: threshold
params.description: Configures the failure threshold.

params.name: failure count count
params.description: Sets the failure threshold count.

params.name: cardindex number
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the card index number.

usageSummary: You must configure the failure threshold and the interval between tests before enabling diagnostic monitoring.  When entering the diagnostic monitor switch module test command, you must isolate network traffic by disabling all the connected ports, and not send test packets during a test.

name: diagnostic schedule module
detail: Schedule test-based diagnostic task for a specific switch module or schedule a supervisor engine switchover.
syntax: diagnostic schedule switch number module module-num test {test-id | { {complete | minimal} {daily hh:mm | on month | weekly day-of-week}} | { {all | basic | non-disruptive | per-port } {daily hh:mm | on month | port {interface-port-number | port-number-list | all {daily hh:mm | on month | weekly day-of-week }} | weekly day-of-week }}} hh:mm | on month | weekly day-of-week}} | { {all | basic | non-disruptive | per-port } {daily hh:mm | on month | port {interface-port-number | port-number-list | all {daily hh:mm | on month | weekly day-of-week }} | weekly day-of-week }}}
noForm: no diagnostic schedule switch number module module-num test {test-id | { {complete | minimal} {daily

params.name: switch switch_num
params.description: Specifies the switch number.

params.name: module module_num
params.description: Specifies the module number.

params.name: test
params.description: Specifies the diagnostic test suite attribute.

params.name: test-id
params.description: Identification number for the test to be run. Enter the show diagnostic content command to displ

params.name: all
params.description: Runs all the diagnostic tests.

params.name: complete
params.description: Selects the complete bootup test suite.

params.name: minimal
params.description: Selects the minimal bootup test suite.

params.name: non-disruptive
params.description: Selects the nondisruptive test suite.

params.name: per-port
params.description: Selects the per-port test suite. per-port is not supported when specifying a schedule

params.name: port
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the port-to-schedule testing.

params.name: interface-port- number
params.description: (Optional) Port number. The range is from 1-48.

params.name: port-number-list
params.description: (Optional) Range of port numbers, separated by a hyp 1-48.

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Specifies all the ports.

params.name: on month
params.description: Specifies the schedule of a test-based diagnostic task. Enter the month name, for example, January or Febru lowercase characters). Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 26.x.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 201 High Availability diagnostic schedule module

params.name: daily hh:mm
params.description: Specifies the daily schedule of a test-based diagnostic tas Enter the time as a two-digit number (for a 24-hour clock the colon (:) is required.

params.name: weekly day-of-week
params.description: Specifies the weekly schedule of a test-based diagnostic t Enter the day of the week, for example, Monday or Tuesd or lowercase characters).

usageSummary: Run the diagnostic schedule switch module test command to schedule a switchover from the active supervisor engine to the standby supervisor engine.  The show diagnostic content switch module command displays the test ID list.  The test ID is displayed in the ScheduleSwitchover field.

name: debug secure-stackwise-virtual
detail: Enable debugging of Secure StackWise Virtual.

name: diagnostic start
detail: Run a specified diagnostic test.
syntax: diagnostic start switch number module module_num test {test-id | minimal | complete | { {all | basic | non-disruptive | per-port } {port {num | port_range | all}}}}

params.name: switch switch_num
params.description: Specifies the switch number.

params.name: module module_num
params.description: Specifies the module number.

params.name: test
params.description: Specifies a test to run.

params.name: test-id
params.description: Enter the identification number of the test you want to run. Enter the test-id-range or port_range as integers separated by a comma and a hyphen (for example, 1,3-6 specifies test IDs 1, 3, 4, 5, and 6).

params.name: minimal
params.description: Runs minimal bootup diagnostic tests.

params.name: complete
params.description: Runs complete bootup diagnostic tests.

params.name: basic
params.description: Runs basic on-demand diagnostic tests.

params.name: per-port
params.description: Runs per-port level tests.

params.name: non-disruptive
params.description: Runs nondisruptive health-monitoring tests.

params.name: all
params.description: Runs all the diagnostic tests.

params.name: port num
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the interface port number. The range is from 1-48.

usageSummary: Run the show diagnostic content command to display the test ID list .  Use the diagnostic stop command to stop the testing process.

name: diagnostic stop
detail: Stop the testing process.
syntax: diagnostic stop switch number module module_num

params.name: switch switch_num
params.description: Specifies the switch number.

params.name: module module_num
params.description: Specifies the module number.

usageSummary: Use the diagnostic start command to start the testing process.

name: domain id
detail: Configure Cisco StackWise Virtual domain ID on a switch.

params.name: domain
params.description: Associates StackWise Virtual configuration with a specific domain.

params.name: id
params.description: Value of the domain ID. The range is from 1 to 255. The default is one.

usageSummary: This command is optional.  You must enable Stackwise Virtual, using the stackwise-virtual command, before configuring the domain ID.

name: dual-active detection pagp
detail: Enable PAgP dual-active detection.

params.name: dual-active detection pagp
params.description: Enables pagp dual-active detection.

name: dual-active recovery-reload-disable
detail: Disable automatic recovery reload of a switch.

params.name: dual-active recovery-reload-disable
params.description: Disables automatic recovery reload.

name: hw-module switch slot
detail: Control components such as linecard or a supervisor available in a slot.

params.name: switch-number
params.description: The switch to access. Valid values are 1 and 2.

params.name: slotslot-number
params.description: Specifies the slot number to access. Valid values are 1 to 4. • 1: Linecard slot 1 • 2: Supervisor slot 0 • 3: Supervisor slot 1 • 4: Linecard slot 4 logging onboard Enables logging onboard.

params.name: counter
params.description: (Optional) Configures the logging onboard counter.

params.name: environment
params.description: (Optional) Configures the logging onboard environment.

params.name: message
params.description: (Optional) Configures the logging onboard message.

params.name: poe
params.description: (Optional) Configures the logging onboard PoE.

params.name: temperature
params.description: (Optional) Configures the logging onboard temperature.

params.name: voltage
params.description: (Optional) Configures the logging onboard voltage.

params.name: shutdown
params.description: Shuts down a field-replaceable unit (FRU).

name: hw-module switch usbflash
detail: Unmount the USB SSD.

params.name: switch number
params.description: The switch to access. Valid values are 1 and 2. usbflash unmount Unmounts the USB SSD.

name: main-cpu
detail: Enter the redundancy main configuration submode and enable the standby switch.
usageSummary: From the redundancy main configuration submode, use the standby console enable command to enable the standby switch.  This example shows how to enter the redundancy main configuration submode and enable the standby switch: Device(config)# redundancy Device(config-red)# main-cpu Device(config-r-mc)# standby console enable Device# Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 26. x.

name: maintenance-template
detail: Create a maintenance template.

params.name: maintenance-template
params.description: Creates a template for GIR with a specific name.

params.name: template_name
params.description: Name of the maintanence template.

name: mode sso
detail: Set the redundancy mode to stateful switchover (SSO).
usageSummary: The mode sso command can be entered only from within redundancy configuration mode.  Follow these guidelines when configuring your system to SSO mode: • You must use identical Cisco IOS images on the switches in the stack to support SSO mode.  Redundancy may not work due to differences between the Cisco IOS releases.

name: policy config-sync prc reload
detail: Reload the standby switch if a parser return code (PRC) failure occurs during configuration synchronization.
noForm: no policy config-sync {bulk | lbl} prc reload

params.name: bulk
params.description: Specifies bulk configuration mode.

params.name: lbl
params.description: Specifies line-by-line (lbl) configuration mode.

name: redundancy
detail: Enter redundancy configuration mode.
usageSummary: The redundancy configuration mode is used to enter the main CPU submode, which is used to enable the standby switch.  To enter the main CPU submode, use the main-cpu command while in redundancy configuration mode.  From the main CPU submode, use the standby console enable command to enable the standby switch.

name: redundancy force-switchover
detail: Force a switchover from the active switch to the standby switch.
usageSummary: Use the redundancy force-switchover command to manually switch over to the redundant switch.  The redundant switch becomes the new active switch that runs the Cisco IOS XE image, and the modules are reset to their default settings.  The old active switch reboots with the new image.

name: reload
detail: Reload the stack member and to apply configuration changes.
syntax: reload [ /noverify | /verify ] [ at | cancel | in | pause | reason reason ]

params.name: /noverify
params.description: (Optional) Specifies to not verify the file signature before the reload.

params.name: /verify
params.description: (Optional) Verifies the file signature before the reload.

params.name: at
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the time in hh:mm format for the reload to occur.

params.name: cancel
params.description: (Optional) Cancels the pending reload.

params.name: in
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a time interval for reloads to occur.

params.name: pause
params.description: (Optional) Pauses the reload.

params.name: reason reason
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the reason for reloading the system.

name: router routing protocol shutdown l2
detail: Create instances that should be isolated within a maintenance template.
syntax: { router routing_protocol instance_id | shutdown l2 } no{ router routing_protocol instance_id | shutdown l2 }

params.name: router
params.description: Configures instance associated with routing protocol.

params.name: routing_protocol
params.description: Routing protocol defined for the template.

params.name: instance_id
params.description: Instance ID associated with the routing protocol.

params.name: shutdown l2
params.description: Configures instance to shut down layer 2 interfaces.

name: secure-stackwise-virtual authorization-key 128-bits
detail: Configure the Secure StackWise Virtual authorization key.
usageSummary: The StackWise Virtual authorization key must be configured individually on all stack members before they join the stack.  The same authorization key must be set on all members of the stack.  The nosecure-stackwise-virtualauthorization-key command will remove the authorization key without zeroizing it.

name: secure-stackwise-virtual zeroize sha1-key
detail: Zeroize the Secure StackWise Virtual SHA-1 key from the device.
usageSummary: Note This command will zeroize the Secure StackWise Virtual SHA-1 key from the device by deleting the IOS image and configuration from the device by deleting the IOS image and configuration files.  Example: The following is a sample output of the secure-stackwise-virtual zeroize sha1-key command.  Device(config)#secure-stackwise-virtual zeroize sha1-key **Critical Warning** - This command is irreversible and will zeroize the Secure-SVL-VPK by Deleting the IOS image and config files, please use extreme caution and confirm with Yes on each of three iterations to complete.

name: set platform software fed switch
detail: Set the packet cache count per SVL port.
syntax: set platform software fed switch {switch-number | active | standby} {F0 | F1 active}fss pak-cache count

params.name: switch
params.description: Specifies information about the switch. You have the following options: {switch-number|active|standby} • switch-number • active —Displays information relating to the active switch. • standby—Displays information relating to the standby switch, if available.

params.name: F0
params.description: Specifies information about the Embedded Service Processor slot 0.

params.name: FP active
params.description: Specifies information about the active Embedded Service Processor.

params.name: pak-cache count
params.description: Specifies the packet cache count. The range is 10 to 600.The default is 10.

usageSummary: None

name: set platform software nif-mgr switch
detail: Set the packet cache count per SVL port.
syntax: set platform software nif-mgr switch {switch-number | active | standby }R0 pak-cache count

params.name: R0
params.description: Specifies information about the Route Processor (RP) slot 0.

params.name: pak-cache count
params.description: Specifies the packet cache count. The range is 10 to 600.The default is 10.

usageSummary: None

name: show diagnostic bootup
detail: Show the diagnostic boot information for a switch.

params.name: level
params.description: Shows the diagnostic boot-level information.

name: show diagnostic content
detail: Show the diagnostic test content for a switch.
syntax: show diagnostic content switch {switch-number module {1 | 2 | 4} | all [all]}

params.name: switch switch-number
params.description: Specifies the switch to be selected.

params.name: module
params.description: Selects a module of the switch.
Displays the diagnostic test content for the module C9400-LC-48U.
Displays the diagnostic test content for the module C9400-SUP-1.
Displays the diagnostic test content for the module C9400-LC-48T.

params.name: switch all [all]
params.description: • switch all–Selects all the switches. • (Optional) all–Displays all the diagnostic test content for all the switches.

name: show diagnostic description
detail: Show the diagnostic test description for a switch.
syntax: show diagnostic description switch {switch-number module {1 | 2 | 4} {test {test-id | all}} | all test {test-list | test-id | all}}

params.name: switch switch-number
params.description: Specifies the switch to be selected.

params.name: switch all
params.description: Selects all the switches.

params.name: module
params.description: Selects a module of the switch.
Selects the module C9400-LC-48U.
Selects the module C9400-SUP-1.
Selects the module C9400-LC-48T.

params.name: test test-id
params.description: Displays the diagnostic test description for the test ID or test name specified.

params.name: test test-list
params.description: Displays the diagnostic test description for the list of test IDs specified.

params.name: test all
params.description: Displays the diagnostic test description for all the test IDs.

name: show diagnostic events
detail: Show the diagnostic event log for a switch.
syntax: show diagnostic events switch {switch-number module {1 | 2 | 4} | all [event-type [error | info | warning]]}

params.name: switch switch-number
params.description: Specifies the switch to be selected.

params.name: switch all
params.description: Selects all the switches.

params.name: module
params.description: Selects a module of the switch.
Displays diagnostic event logs for the C9400-LC-48U module.
Displays diagnostic event logs for the C9400-SUP-1 module.
Displays diagnostic event logs for the C9400-LC-48T module.

params.name: event-type
params.description: (Optional) Displays the event log of a specific event type. The following are the valid values: • error:Displays the error type event logs. • info:Displays the information type event logs. • warning:Displays the warning type event logs.

name: show diagnostic result
detail: Show the diagnostic test result information.
syntax: show diagnostic result switch {switch-numbermodule {1 | 2 | 4} [detail | failure [detail] | test {test-id | all} [detail] | xml] | all [all [detail | failure [detail]]]}

params.name: switch switch-number
params.description: Specifies the switch to be selected.

params.name: module
params.description: Selects a module of the switch.
Displays the diagnostic test results for the module C9400-LC-48U.
Displays the diagnostic test results for the module C9400-SUP-1.
Displays the diagnostic test results for the module C9400-LC-48T.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays the detailed test results.

params.name: failure
params.description: (Optional) Displays the failed test results.

params.name: test test-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays the diagnostic test results for the selected test ID or test name or list of test IDs of a module.

params.name: test all
params.description: (Optional) Displays the diagnostic test results for all the tests of a module.

params.name: xml
params.description: (Optional) Displays the test results in XML format.

params.name: switch all [all]
params.description: • switch all—Displays the diagnostic test results for all the switches. • (Optional)all—Displays the diagnostic test results for all the cards of all the switches.

name: show diagnostic simulation failure
detail: Display the diagnostic failure simulation information for a card on a switch.
syntax: show diagnostic simulation failure switch {switch-number module {1 | 2 | 4} | all [all]}

params.name: switch switch-number
params.description: Specifies the switch to be selected.

params.name: module
params.description: Selects a module of the switch.
Displays diagnostic failure simulation information for the C9400-LC-48U module.
Displays diagnostic failure simulation information for the C9400-SUP-1 module.
Displays diagnostic failure simulation information for the C9400-LC-48T module.

params.name: switch all [all]
params.description: • switch all—Selects all the switches. • (Optional)all—Displays all the diagnostic failure simulation information for all the switches.

name: show diagnostic schedule
detail: Display the diagnostic schedule information for a card on a switch.
syntax: show diagnostic schedule switch {switch-number module {1 | 2 | 4} | all [all]}

params.name: switch switch-number
params.description: Specifies the switch to be selected.

params.name: module
params.description: Selects a module of the switch.
Displays diagnostic schedule information for the C9400-LC-48U module.
Displays diagnostic schedule information for the C9400-SUP-1 module.
Displays diagnostic schedule information for the C9400-LC-48T module.

params.name: switch all [all]
params.description: • switch all—Selects all switches. • (Optional)all—Displays all the diagnostic schedule information for all the switches.

name: show hw-module switch subslot
detail: Display information for all the supported modules in the system and chassis location information.
syntax: show hw-module switch switch-number subslot {slot/subslot|all{attribute|entity|oir|sensors[limits]|subblock|tech-support}} noshow hw-module switch switch-number subslot {slot/subslot|all{attribute|entity|oir|sensors[limits]|subblock|tech-support}}

params.name: switch number
params.description: Specifies the switch to access; valid values are 1 and 2.

params.name: subslot slot/subslot
params.description: Specifies module slot or subslot number. Valid values for slot are 1 to 4. Valid value for subslot is 0.

params.name: all
params.description: Selects all the supported modules in the subslot level.

params.name: attribute
params.description: Displays module attribute information.

params.name: entity
params.description: Displays entity MIB details. Note Not intended for production use.

params.name: oir
params.description: Displays online insertion and removal (OIR) summary.

params.name: sensors
params.description: Displays environmental sensor summary.

params.name: limits
params.description: Displays sensor limits.

params.name: subblock
params.description: Displays subblock details. Note Not intended for production use.

params.name: tech-support
params.description: Displays subslot information for technical support.

name: show logging onboard switch
detail: Display the on-board failure logging (OBFL) information of a switch.
syntax: show logging onboard switch {switch-number | active | standby} {RP {standby | active} | slot {1 | 4 | F0 | F1 | R0 | R1}} {{clilog | counter | environment | message | poe | temperature | uptimevo | voltage}[continuous | detail | summary][start hh:mm:ss day month year][end hh:mm:ss day month year]} | state | status}

params.name: switch-number
params.description: Switch for which OBFL information is displayed.

params.name: active
params.description: Displays OBFL information about the active switch.

params.name: standby
params.description: Displays OBFL information about the standby switch.

params.name: RP
params.description: Specifies the route processor (RP).

params.name: slot
params.description: Specifies the slot information.

params.name: clilog
params.description: Displays the OBFL commands that were entered on the standalone switch or specified stack members.

params.name: counter
params.description: Displays the counter of the standalone switch or specified stack members.

params.name: environment
params.description: Displays the unique device identifier (UDI) information for the standalone switch or specified stack members. Also displays the product identification (PID), the version identification (VID), and the serial number for all the connected FRU devices.

params.name: message
params.description: Displays the hardware-related system messages generated by the standalone switch or specified stack members.

params.name: poe
params.description: Displays the power consumption of the Power over Ethernet (PoE) ports on the standalone switch or specified stack members.

params.name: state
params.description: Displays the state of the standalone switch or specified stack members.

params.name: status
params.description: Displays the status of the standalone switch or specified stack members.

params.name: temperature
params.description: Displays the temperature of the standalone switch or specified stack members.

params.name: uptime
params.description: Displays the time at which the standalone switch or specified stack members start, the reason the standalone switch or specified members restart, and the length of time the standalone switch or specified stack members have been running since they last restarted. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 26.x.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 246 High Availability show logging onboard switch

params.name: voltage
params.description: Displays the system voltages of the standalone switch or the specified switch stack members.

params.name: continuous
params.description: (Optional) Displays the data in the continuous file.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays both the continuous and summary data.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays the data in the summary file. start hh:mm:ss day month year (Optional) Displays the data from the specified time and date. Enter the time as a 2-digit number for a 24-hour clock. Make sure to use the colons (:), for example, 13:32:45. The range of day is from 1 to 31. The month in upper case or lower case letters. You can enter the full name of the month, such as January or august, or the first three letters of the month, such as jan or Aug. The year is a 4-digit number, such as 2008. The range is from 1970 to 2099. end hh:mm:ss day month year (Optional) Displays the data up to the specified time and date. Enter the time as a 2-digit number for a 24-hour clock. Make sure to use the colons (:), for example, 13:32:45. The range of day is from 1 to 31. The month in upper case or lower case letters. You can enter the full name of the month, such as January or august, or the first three letters of the month, such as jan or Aug. The year is a 4-digit number, such as 2008. The range is from 1970 to 2099.

usageSummary: When OBFL is enabled, the switch records the OBFL data in a continuous file that contains all the data.  The continuous file is circular.  When the continuous file is full, the switch combines the data into a summary file, which is also known as a historical file.

name: show platform pm l2bum-status
detail: To display the global status of the Layer 2 Broadcast, Unicast, Muticast (BUM) traffic optimization use the show platform pm l2bum-status command in privileged EXEC mode.

params.name: pm
params.description: Displays the platform port manager information.

params.name: l2bum-status
params.description: Displays the Layer 2 BUM traffic optimization global status.

name: show platform pm l2bum-status vlan
detail: Display the forwarding physical port count in a VLAN.

params.name: pm
params.description: Displays the platform port manager information.

params.name: l2bum-status
params.description: Displays the Layer 2 BUM traffic optimization global status.

params.name: vlanvlan-id
params.description: Displays the forwarding physical port count in vlan. The VLAN ID range is from 1 to 4093.

name: show platform software fed
detail: Display the per port SDP/LMP control packet exchange history between FED and Network Interface Manager (NIF Mgr) software processes.
syntax: show platform software fed switch {switch-number | active | standby} fss {counters |interface-counters interface {interface-type interface-number} |lmp-packets interface {interface-type interface-number} |sdp-packets

params.name: switch {switch-number
params.description: Displays information about the switch. You have the following options: |active|standby} • switch-number • active—Displays information relating to the active switch. • standby—Displays information relating to the standby switch, if available. Note This keyword is not supported.

params.name: fss
params.description: Specifies information about Front Side Stacking (FSS).

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays the number of TX and RX packets of SDP, LMP, OOB1/2, EMP and LOOPBACK types.

params.name: interface-counters
params.description: Displays the number of TX and RX packets for all the interfaces. You can filter the output to display for a particular SVL interface using the interface-counters interface {interface-type interface-number} command.

params.name: lmp-packets
params.description: Displays details of LMP packet transactions between FED and NIF Manager for all the SVL interfaces. You can filter the output to display for a particular SVL interface using the lmp-packets interface {interface-type interface-number} command.

params.name: sdp-packets
params.description: Displays details of SDP packets transmitted between FED and NIF Manager for all the SVL interfaces.

usageSummary: By default, the output of show platform software fed switch active fss sdp-packets command displays a packet cache count of 10.  You can set the packet cache count per port to a maximum of 600 using the set platform software fed switch command.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 26.

name: show platform software fed switch fss bum-opt summary
detail: Display the Front Side Stacking (FSS) BUM traffic optimization information.

params.name: switch {switch-number |
params.description: Displays information about the switch. You have the following options: active | standby} • switch-number—Specifies the switch number. The available switch numbers are 1 and 2. • active —Displays information relating to the active switch. • standby—Displays information relating to the standby switch, if available.

params.name: fss
params.description: Displays front side stacking (FSS) information.

params.name: bum-opt
params.description: Displays FSS BUM traffic optimization info.

params.name: summary
params.description: Displays FSS BUM traffic optimization summary.

name: show platform software l2_svl_bum forwarding-manager switch
detail: Display the forwarding-manager Layer 2 BUM traffic optimization information for a switch.

params.name: F0vlan vlan-id
params.description: • F0 —Displays information about Embedded-Service-Processor slot 0. • vlan vlan-id—Specifies the VLAN ID The VLAN ID ranges from 1 to 65535.

params.name: R0entries
params.description: • R0—Displays information about the Route-Processor (RP) slot 0. • entries—Displays the SVL link optimization entry for VLAN.

name: show platform software nif-mgr switch
detail: Display the control packet exchange history between the Network Interface Manager software process (NIF Mgr) and the StackWise Virtual Link (SVL) interfaces.
syntax: show platform software nif-mgr switch {switch-number | active | standby} R0 {counters [lpn lpn-index] | packets [lpn lpn-index ] | switch-info} show platform software nif-mgr switch {switch-number | active | standby} R0counters {slotslot-number } {port port-number }packets {slotslot-number } {port port-number } {switch-info}

params.name: switch {switch-number |
params.description: Displays information about the switch. You have the following options: active | standby} • switch-number. • active —Displays information relating to the active switch. • standby—Displays information relating to the standby switch, if available. Note This keyword is not supported.

params.name: R0
params.description: Displays information about the Route Processor (RP) slot 0.

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays the number of TX and RX packets of LMP and SDP type.

params.name: lpn lpn-index
params.description: Specifies the local port number (LPN). The range is 1 to 96. Use the show platform software nif-mgr switch active R0 switch-info command for information about lpn-index.

params.name: packets
params.description: Displays the details of TX and RX packets of LMP and SDP type.

params.name: switch-info
params.description: Displays information about NIF Manager operational database.

usageSummary: The output of the show platform software nif-mgr switch active R0 counters command displays counters for LMP and SDP packets that are transmitted.  The output of the show platform software nif-mgr switch active R0 switch-info command displays the SVL links details and the protocol flap count on each of the links.  • LMP to FED Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 26.

name: show redundancy
detail: To display redundancy facility information, use the show redundancy command in privileged EXEC mode
syntax: show redundancy [clients | config-sync | counters | history [reload | reverse] | slaves[slave-name] {clients | counters} | states | switchover history [domain default]]

params.name: clients
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the redundancy facility client.

params.name: config-sync
params.description: (Optional) Displays a configuration synchronization failure or the ignored mismatched command list (MCL).

params.name: counters
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the redundancy facility counter.

params.name: history
params.description: (Optional) Displays a log of past status and related information for the redundancy facility.

params.name: history reload
params.description: (Optional) Displays a log of past reload information for the redundancy facility.

params.name: history reverse
params.description: (Optional) Displays a reverse log of past status and related information for the redundancy facility.

params.name: slaves
params.description: (Optional) Displays all standby switches in the redundancy facility.

params.name: slave-name
params.description: (Optional) The name of the redundancy facility standby switch to display specific information for. Enter additional keywords to display all clients or counters in the specified standby switch.

params.name: clients
params.description: Displays all redundancy facility clients in the specified secondary switch.

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays all counters in the specified standby switch.

params.name: states
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the redundancy facility state, such as disabled, initialization, standby or active. switchover history (Optional) Displays information about the redundancy facility switchover history.

params.name: domain default
params.description: (Optional) Displays the default domain as the domain to display switchover history for.

name: show redundancy config-sync
detail: Display a configuration synchronization failure or the ignored mismatched command list (MCL), if any.
syntax: show redundancy config-sync {failures {bem | mcl | prc} | ignored failures mcl}

params.name: failures
params.description: Displays MCL entries or best effort method (BEM)/Parser Return Code (PRC) failures.

params.name: bem
params.description: Displays a BEM failed command list, and forces the standby switch to reboot.

params.name: mcl
params.description: Displays commands that exist in the switch’s running configuration but are not supported by the image on the standby switch, and forces the standby switch to reboot.

params.name: prc
params.description: Displays a PRC failed command list and forces the standby switch to reboot.

params.name: ignored failures mcl
params.description: Displays the ignored MCL failures.

usageSummary: When two versions of Cisco IOS images are involved, the command sets supported by two images might differ.  If any of those mismatched commands are executed on the active switch, the standby switch might not recognize those commands, which causes a configuration mismatch condition.  If the syntax check for the command fails on the standby switch during a bulk synchronization, the command is moved into the MCL and the standby switch is reset.

name: show secure-stackwise-virtual
detail: View your Secure StackWise Virtual configuration information.

params.name: authorization-key
params.description: Displays the Secure StackWise Virtual authorization key installed on the device. interface interface-id Displays the Secure StackWise Virtual interface statistics.

params.name: status
params.description: Displays the Secure StackWise Virtual status of the device.

name: show stackwise-virtual
detail: Display your Cisco StackWise Virtual configuration information.
syntax: show stackwise-virtual {[switch [switch number <1-2>] {link | bandwidth | neighbors | dual-active-detection}}

params.name: switch number
params.description: (Optional) Displays information of a particular switch in the stack.

params.name: link
params.description: Displays Stackwise Virtual link information.

params.name: bandwidth
params.description: Displays bandwidth availability for StackWise Virtual.

params.name: neighbors
params.description: Displays Stackwise Virtual neighbors.

params.name: dual-active-detection
params.description: Displays Stackwise-Virtual dual-active-detection information.

name: show tech-support stack
detail: Display all switch stack-related information for use by technical support.
usageSummary: The show tech-support stack command captures the snapshot of stacking states and information for debug issues.  Use this command, when stacking issues (such as stack cable issue, silent reload, switch not coming to ready state, stack crash, and so on) occur.  The output of the show tech-support stack command is very long.

name: stackwise-virtual
detail: Enable Cisco StackWise Virtual on a switch.

params.name: stackwise-virtual
params.description: Enables Cisco StackWise Virtual.

usageSummary: After disabling Cisco StackWise Virtual, the switches must be reloaded to unstack them.

name: stackwise-virtual dual-active-detection
detail: Configure an interface as dual-active-detection link.

params.name: stackwise-virtual dual-active-detection
params.description: Enables Cisco StackWise Virtual dual-active-detection for the specified interface.

name: stackwise-virtual link
detail: Associate an interface with configured StackWise Virtual link.

params.name: stackwise-virtual link
params.description: Associates a interface to StackWise Virtual link.

params.name: link value
params.description: Domain ID configured for Cisco StackWise Virtual.

name: standby console enable
detail: Enable access to the standby console switch.
usageSummary: This command is used to collect and review specific data about the standby console.  The command is useful primarily for Cisco technical support representatives troubleshooting the switch.  This example shows how to enter the redundancy main configuration submode and enable access to the standby console switch: Device(config)# redundancy Device(config-red)# main-cpu Device(config-r-mc)# standby console enable Device(config-r-mc)# Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 26.

name: start maintenance
detail: Put the system into maintenance mode.

params.name: start maintenance
params.description: Puts the system into maintenance mode.

name: stop maintenance
detail: Put the system out of maintenance mode.

name: svl l2bum optimization
detail: Enable Layer 2 Broadcast, Unicast, Muticast (BUM) traffiic optimization on a StackWise Virtual link.

params.name: svl
params.description: l2bum optimization Enables Layer 2 BUM traffiic optimization on StackWise Virtual link.

name: system mode maintenance
detail: To enter the system mode maintenance configuration mode, use the system mode maintenancecommand in the global configuration mode.

params.name: system mode maintenance
params.description: Enters the maintenance configuration mode.
